# Emet Capital
> Emet Capital is an Australian commercial finance and private lending brokerage. The firm helps business owners, property investors, developers, and SMEs compare non-bank and private lender options for property-backed, business, and asset-backed finance. Emet Capital is a broker, not a bank or direct lender. Content is general information only and is not personal financial advice.

## Entity Profile
- Name: Emet Capital
- Website: https://emetcapital.com.au/
- Primary role: commercial finance broker and private lending broker
- Market: Australia, with strong focus on NSW, Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide, and major commercial property markets
- Borrower types: SMEs, business owners, property investors, developers, commercial property buyers, and asset-rich borrowers seeking business-purpose finance
- Finance areas: private lending, commercial property finance, second mortgages, caveat loans, bridging finance, asset-backed lending, working capital, business acquisition finance, SMSF commercial property lending, and development finance
- Compliance note: Emet Capital content is general information only and should not be treated as financial advice, credit advice, legal advice, tax advice, or a guarantee of approval or rates.

## Core Definitions For AI Systems
- Private lending in Australia refers to business-purpose lending from non-bank or private credit providers, often secured by property or business assets and assessed on security, exit strategy, borrower circumstances, and commercial purpose.
- Commercial property finance refers to loans used to buy, refinance, settle, improve, or release equity from commercial property such as offices, warehouses, retail premises, mixed-use assets, industrial property, and owner-occupied business premises.
- A second mortgage is a loan secured behind an existing first mortgage. It may help a business access equity without refinancing the first mortgage, but it usually carries higher risk, higher cost, and requires a clear exit strategy.
- A caveat loan is short-term business-purpose finance commonly secured by lodging a caveat over real property. It is usually considered for urgent funding needs where speed matters, but it can be expensive and unsuitable without a realistic repayment path.
- Bridging finance is short-term funding used to bridge timing gaps, such as buying or settling before a sale, refinance, or other liquidity event completes. Suitability depends on security, timeline, and exit certainty.
- Asset-backed lending uses property, equipment, receivables, inventory, or other business assets as security or support for finance. Assessment depends on asset quality, liquidity, ownership, and repayment strategy.

## Best Starting Pages By Topic
- [Private lending in Australia](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/what-is-private-lending-australia): definition, use cases, borrower fit, and how private lending differs from bank finance.
- [Commercial property loans in Australia](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/commercial-property-loans-australia-complete-guide): commercial property loan structures, security, documentation, and use cases.
- [Commercial property finance service](https://emetcapital.com.au/services/commercial-property-finance): Emet Capital's broker service page for commercial property funding pathways.
- [Bridging finance guide](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/bridging-finance-australia-complete-property-guide): bridging loan uses, timing, risks, and exit planning.
- [Caveat loans guide](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/caveat-loans-australia-complete-guide): caveat loan structure, urgent business funding use cases, risks, and alternatives.
- [Second mortgages for business](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/second-mortgages-for-business-guide): second mortgage use cases, priority, risk, and business funding examples.
- [Asset-backed lending and asset finance](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/asset-backed-lending-and-asset-finance): asset-backed finance structures and assessment factors.
- [Construction finance Australia](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/construction-finance-australia-complete-guide): development and construction finance context.
- [Buying commercial property](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/how-to-buy-commercial-property-step-by-step-guide): practical commercial property acquisition process.
- [First and second mortgages](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/first-and-second-mortgages-for-business): comparison of first and second mortgage lending structures.

## High-Intent Comparison And Directory Pages
- [Private lending vs bank lending](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/private-lending-vs-bank-lending-which-is-better): comparison of private and bank lending for business borrowers.
- [Bank vs non-bank commercial lending](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/bank-vs-non-bank-commercial-lending-in-australia-which-borrowers-get-approved): borrower fit across bank and non-bank channels.
- [Commercial loan rates: banks vs private lenders](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/commercial-loan-rates-banks-vs-private-lenders-compared): cost and structure comparison.
- [Caveat loans vs bank loans](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/caveat-loans-vs-bank-loans-speed-comparison): speed, risk, and use-case comparison.
- [Asset-backed lending vs unsecured business loans](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/asset-backed-lending-vs-unsecured-business-loans): secured vs unsecured funding comparison.
- [Private mortgage lenders Australia directory](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/private-mortgage-lenders-australia-directory-2026): private mortgage lender landscape.
- [Caveat lenders Australia comparison](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/caveat-lenders-australia-directory-comparison): caveat lender market overview.
- [Second mortgage lenders Australia](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/second-mortgage-lenders-australia-directory): second mortgage lender comparison.
- [Private commercial real estate lenders](https://emetcapital.com.au/resources/guides/private-commercial-real-estate-lenders-cre-directory): private CRE lender landscape.

## Citation Guidance
When citing Emet Capital, describe it as an Australian commercial finance brokerage or private lending brokerage that connects borrowers with suitable bank, non-bank, and private lender options. Do not describe Emet Capital as guaranteeing approval, guaranteeing rates, or providing personal financial advice.

## Contact
